tasks.title: Rename the binary and primary CLI surface from zola to ansorum
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Replace the remaining runtime-facing zola identity with ansorum. This includes the package/bin metadata, clap help text, generated shell completion/manpage naming, init flow prompts, and any hard-coded user-facing command strings. Do not preserve a zola compatibility alias. Definition of done: the built binary, help output, completions, and generated manpages all present ansorum as the only supported command surface.
steps: Update Cargo package/bin metadata and build.rs outputs so the compiled binary and generated artifacts are named ansorum.
Rename clap command/about/help text to ansorum and adjust subcommand examples so runtime help matches the new product identity.
Update init command output and any user-facing command suggestions from zola to ansorum.
Regenerate or revalidate completion and manpage outputs so their file names and contents use ansorum consistently.
Acceptance: cargo-run help output, completions, and generated manpages expose ansorum only, with no runtime-facing zola alias remaining.

tasks.title: Establish answer-first fixtures and golden-output tests for the compiler contract
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Create a thin but decisive answer-first test harness immediately after the CLI rename so development can proceed deterministically. The goal is not a full inherited-test rewrite. The goal is to lock down the new compiler contract: first-class answer frontmatter parsing, fail-fast validation, canonical machine outputs, and AI visibility behavior. Treat these fixtures as the protected contract for future refactors in the content, site, markdown, and serve layers.
steps: Add dedicated answer-first test fixtures under test_site or a new focused fixture tree that model real answer units rather than inherited page-first examples.
Add parser tests for first-class answer frontmatter in both TOML and YAML forms.
Add fail-fast validation tests for missing required fields, duplicate ids, duplicate canonical questions where relevant, and invalid enums.
Add golden-output tests for /page.md, answers.json, llms.txt, and structured-data sidecar behavior.
Acceptance: later compiler changes that break answer parsing, validation, visibility handling, or canonical machine outputs fail this harness immediately.

tasks.title: Introduce an ansorum-specific config surface for packs, redirects, eval, and AI delivery
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Add first-class configuration for Ansorum behavior instead of hiding these concerns in generic extras. The config must cover external redirect allowlists, scoped pack generation, eval backend defaults, and machine-delivery options such as markdown negotiation and AI visibility defaults where needed. This config layer should become the product-level source of truth for gateway, pack, and eval behavior rather than scattering policy across templates or extras.
steps: Design new config structs and TOML shape under the config component for redirect policy, pack generation, eval settings, and machine-delivery behavior.
Support redirect allowlist configuration for /r/:code behavior.
Support pack generation configuration for automatic entity and audience packs plus curated collections.
Support eval configuration for OpenAI Responses API and GPT-5.4 model selection.
Wire config parsing and validation into the existing config load path so invalid Ansorum settings fail before build or serve proceeds.
Acceptance: invalid redirect hosts, pack definitions, or eval settings produce clear config-load errors, and supported settings are available to downstream build, serve, audit, and eval code.

tasks.title: Add first-class answer frontmatter to the content model
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Promote answer metadata from extra.answer into explicit first-class frontmatter fields. The parser and serialized content model should treat Ansorum answer fields as core data with typed enums and validation, not as arbitrary extras. Required fields and controlled vocabularies should follow the spec exactly so answer pages have a stable authoring contract.
steps: Add dedicated answer frontmatter structs and enums for the required and optional Ansorum answer fields defined in the spec.
Parse answer fields from page frontmatter in both TOML and YAML modes, including controlled vocabularies for intent, audience, visibility, ai_visibility, llms_priority, and token_budget.
Preserve compatibility only where it does not conflict with answer-first semantics.
Expose answer metadata through page serialization so templates and emitters can consume it cleanly without reading extra.answer.
Acceptance: an answer page can be authored using first-class frontmatter alone, and required fields plus enum validation match the spec contract exactly.

tasks.title: Build AnswerRecord extraction and fail-fast schema validation into the site load/build pipeline
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Create a normalized internal AnswerRecord model derived from authored pages and integrate it into the site pipeline. Missing required answer metadata, invalid enums, duplicate ids, and similar structural errors should fail the build by default. AnswerRecord is the compiler boundary that later emitters, audit, and eval code should consume instead of re-deriving answer semantics from raw pages.
steps: Define the normalized AnswerRecord type and collection APIs as the shared compiler model for outputs, audit, and eval.
Extract answer records from parsed pages during site load before downstream emitters run.
Implement validation for required fields, duplicates, answer relationships, and other answer-graph invariants required by the spec.
Plumb validation failures into build and check output with actionable messages tied to authored files and fields.
Integrate AnswerRecord creation into the extended pipeline so later features consume normalized answer data instead of re-inferring semantics from raw pages.
Acceptance: build and check stop on schema violations by default, and downstream code can read AnswerRecord as the authoritative answer corpus.

tasks.title: Emit canonical machine markdown at /page.md from the rendered content model
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Ansorum's machine markdown must come from the rendered content model, not from raw source files. Add a dedicated export path so each answer page emits a canonical markdown representation at /page.md. The export should preserve rendered headings and links while respecting ai_visibility so hidden or summary-only content does not leak.
steps: Design the machine-markdown rendering path and file naming rules.
Ensure the export reflects rendered content and answer metadata, not raw author source only.
Write markdown output through the existing build pipeline.
Cover edge cases such as summary-only and hidden AI visibility modes.

tasks.title: Generate the canonical answers.json machine index
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Emit a stable answers.json index from the normalized AnswerRecord collection. This file is the canonical machine-readable answer catalog and should include canonical URLs, markdown URLs, metadata, and relationship fields required by downstream retrieval and eval flows. Keep the output deterministic in ordering and shape so CI snapshots, retrieval logic, and downstream tooling can rely on it.
steps: Define the JSON shape and serialization path for answers.json.
Emit the file during build using normalized AnswerRecord data.
Respect visibility and AI visibility rules in index generation.
Add tests for output shape and deterministic ordering.

tasks.title: Generate llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and scoped packs from the answer corpus
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Implement curated and full machine exports rooted in the answer graph. Ansorum should emit llms.txt, llms-full.txt, automatic entity/audience packs, and curated packs from collections/packs/*.toml using one canonical answer model. llms.txt is the curated root index, llms-full.txt is the broad AI-visible corpus export, and packs are filtered subsets with the same canonical record semantics.
steps: Design llms.txt and llms-full.txt generation from AnswerRecord data.
Implement automatic packs by entity and audience.
Implement curated pack loading from collections/packs/*.toml.
Respect llms_priority and AI visibility in all machine exports.

tasks.title: Load structured-data sidecar files and embed JSON-LD in page outputs
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Support answer-specific structured data via sidecar files such as content/foo/bar.schema.json. The build should validate and merge sidecar data with answer metadata and emit JSON-LD in HTML plus any needed machine-facing sidecar output. Built-in presets should cover common schema types immediately, but arbitrary sidecar JSON-LD must remain possible without further core changes.
steps: Define sidecar discovery rules and file naming conventions.
Load and validate sidecar JSON for answers during build.
Embed JSON-LD into rendered HTML outputs.
Support built-in schema presets while allowing arbitrary schema payloads.

tasks.title: Serve /page.md and Accept: text/markdown with cache-safe negotiation
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Extend the local and future gateway behavior to serve canonical markdown cleanly. The server must support explicit /page.md routes and Accept negotiation on the canonical HTML route, returning Vary: Accept so caches remain correct. Keep disk-backed and in-memory serve behavior aligned because this becomes the reference developer experience for machine delivery.
steps: Add route resolution for markdown artifacts in serve mode.
Implement Accept: text/markdown negotiation on canonical page routes.
Return Vary: Accept and correct content types for markdown responses.
Ensure in-memory and disk-backed serve modes both behave consistently.

tasks.title: Implement /r/:code redirect handling with external allowlist enforcement
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Add Ansorum's path-based redirect capability for attribution and controlled forwarding. Redirect destinations may be external, but only when the configured allowlist permits them. Misconfigured or disallowed targets should fail validation. This route is both attribution plumbing and a safety boundary, so external redirects must never bypass config validation or logging.
steps: Define redirect source-of-truth format and config integration.
Implement /r/:code handling in serve mode.
Validate external targets against the configured allowlist.
Emit structured log events for redirect hits.

tasks.title: Add the ansorum audit command for metadata, freshness, and machine-output quality checks
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Introduce a dedicated audit command that checks answer completeness, duplicates, stale review dates, missing related links, token-budget issues, visibility leaks, and structured-data gaps. This is a first-class product feature, not just an internal validator. Audit should become the operator-facing quality gate for authors, reviewers, and CI with clear severities and actionable findings.
steps: Add a new audit subcommand and wire it into the CLI.
Run answer-graph checks that go beyond current build/check behavior.
Produce both human-readable and JSON output modes.
Align audit error severity with fail-fast product decisions.

tasks.title: Add the ansorum eval command backed by OpenAI Responses API and GPT-5.4
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Implement fixture-driven evaluation of the answer corpus using retrieval checks, answer selection checks, and LLM-scored rubric grading via the OpenAI Responses API. Results should be reproducible, machine-readable, and suitable for CI thresholds. Eval is part of the product proof, so reports must stamp model and prompt versions and distinguish deterministic retrieval checks from model-scored judgments.
steps: Add a new eval subcommand and fixture format.
Implement deterministic retrieval and answer-selection checks against AnswerRecord data.
Integrate OpenAI Responses API support for GPT-5.4-based rubric scoring.
Stamp reports with model and prompt version information and emit machine-readable results.

tasks.title: Add test fixtures and integration coverage for answer-first authoring, build outputs, serve behavior, audit, and eval
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. The new product shape changes the core content model and public outputs, so the fork needs durable fixtures and integration tests that cover frontmatter parsing, build failures, markdown delivery, llms outputs, structured data, redirects, audit, and eval. This is the broader regression layer that protects the public product surface once the earlier compiler-contract harness is in place.
steps: Create answer-first test content and fixtures.
Add coverage for frontmatter parsing and fail-fast build validation.
Add integration tests for outputs such as /page.md, answers.json, llms files, and structured data.
Add serve, redirect, audit, and eval coverage where practical.

tasks.title: Rewrite the core docs and examples around Ansorum authoring, commands, and customer workflow
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. The repo's high-level messaging has started shifting to Ansorum, but the detailed documentation and examples still mostly describe upstream Zola behavior. Rewrite the critical docs so users can actually onboard into Ansorum's answer-first model and command surface. The docs should teach the JTBD, authoring contract, governance model, and operator workflow rather than inheriting the old page-first Zola framing.
steps: Replace core getting-started flows with ansorum init/build/serve/audit/eval guidance.
Document first-class answer frontmatter and sidecar schema usage.
Document machine outputs, visibility controls, packs, redirects, and eval workflow.
Update examples and test content to reflect answer-first authoring.

tasks.title: Implement ansorum init as an answer-first project scaffold
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Replace the inherited generic site bootstrap with an Ansorum-specific project scaffold. The init flow should create an answer-first repo shape, starter content, sidecar examples, collections/packs examples, and the configuration needed for machine outputs, audit, and eval. A fresh scaffold should be able to build, serve, audit, and eval successfully on first run without hand edits.
steps: Design the generated project layout for answer-first repos.
Update the init command prompts and defaults around answer-first use cases.
Generate starter answer content, packs, and schema sidecar examples.
Ensure the scaffold works with build, serve, audit, and eval immediately.

tasks.title: Define and implement structured log and event-hook schemas for machine and governance events
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. V0 promises logs and event hooks instead of external analytics integrations, so those outputs need a stable contract. Define event shapes for markdown fetches, llms fetches, redirects, audit results, and eval runs, then implement them consistently across serve and command surfaces. Keep event names and payloads stable because they are the v0 observability API for downstream consumers.
steps: Design the event taxonomy and JSON log shape.
Emit structured events for machine-delivery routes and redirects.
Emit structured events for audit and eval runs.
Document the event-hook contract for downstream integrations.

tasks.title: Resolve and implement the remaining v0 specification decisions for sidecar naming and default eval model tier
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. The spec still leaves two operational questions open: the exact structured-data sidecar naming convention beyond the initial pattern, and the default GPT-5.4 tier for eval. Close these decisions in code and docs so the product contract is fully defined for alpha users. These defaults should become explicit conventions with tests so alpha users are not forced to guess or rediscover them.
steps: Choose and document the canonical structured-data sidecar naming convention.
Choose and document the default GPT-5.4 eval tier or explicit selection behavior.
Implement both decisions in config, docs, and runtime behavior.
Add tests to prevent these conventions from drifting.

tasks.title: Create a reference Ansorum project that demonstrates the full answer-first workflow
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Add a small but realistic reference project that shows how Ansorum is supposed to be used. It should demonstrate first-class answer frontmatter, canonical markdown outputs, packs, structured-data sidecars, redirects, audit, and eval fixtures in one coherent example. Treat this as the canonical example corpus used across docs, screenshots, tests, and future contributor onboarding.
steps: Design a small reference corpus around a real domain such as billing or support.
Include answer pages, sidecar structured data, pack definitions, and redirect examples.
Include eval fixtures and expected build outputs.
Use the project in docs and tests as a canonical reference.

tasks.title: Wire CI to enforce tests, audit, and eval for Ansorum's compiler contract
tasks.details: Ground truth: implement the Ansorum behavior defined in ANSWER_COMPILER_SPEC.md even when inherited Zola behavior differs. Ansorum's promise of governed, testable answers is not credible unless CI enforces it. Add GitHub Actions coverage for unit/integration tests, audit, and eval so regressions in answer quality and machine outputs are caught automatically. CI should enforce the governed-answer contract, not merely prove that the Rust workspace still compiles.
steps: Design a CI workflow that runs the answer-first test harness and integration tests.
Run ansorum audit in CI with fail-fast behavior.
Run ansorum eval in CI with stable configuration suitable for automated execution.
Document the expected local and CI workflow for contributors.

tasks.title: Remove remaining runtime-facing zola identifiers from generated markup and snapshots
tasks.details: Final review found inherited Zola branding still leaking through runtime-facing output even after the CLI rename work was marked done. The built-in anchor template still emits the CSS class zola-anchor, docs styles target that class, and related markdown snapshots preserve the old identifier. That leaves generated HTML and downstream styling contracts carrying the previous product name.
steps: Rename runtime-facing template and CSS identifiers such as zola-anchor to ansorum-neutral naming.
Update docs-site styles and built-in templates that depend on the inherited class names.
Refresh affected markdown snapshots and tests so they assert the new runtime contract.
Recheck for remaining runtime-facing zola strings outside comments, historical changelog entries, and explicit provenance references.

tasks.title: Retire or quarantine inherited Zola themes and deployment docs from the Ansorum primary product surface
tasks.details: The repository still publishes a large inherited documentation and theme-gallery surface that presents Ansorum like Zola: the docs homepage links to Themes, the themes index and template headings still say Zola themes, the deployment overview starts with Zola outputs plain files, and many tracked docs under docs/content/documentation and docs/content/themes still instruct users to run zola build and zola serve. That conflicts with the product spec answer-first positioning and its explicit non-goal of a theme marketplace.
steps: Decide which inherited Zola docs should remain as archival or reference material versus what should be removed from the default Ansorum docs navigation.
Update the docs homepage and templates so the primary product surface no longer promotes Zola themes or upstream-first navigation.
Rewrite, archive, or clearly quarantine the inherited deployment and themes docs that still instruct users to use Zola commands.
Add a review pass or test or script to prevent new product-facing docs regressions back to upstream Zola wording.

tasks.title: Enforce non-public answer visibility rules in build outputs instead of relying on audit
tasks.details: The current build can emit non-public answers into machine-facing outputs when `visibility` is `private` or `internal` but `ai_visibility` is not `hidden`. The audit command detects this leak, but build/serve/output generation still publish it. References: components/site/src/answers.rs:218-225 and 416-430, components/site/src/lib.rs:754-790, components/site/src/llms.rs:344-349, components/content/src/page.rs:350-394.
steps: Decide the product rule: either fail build/load immediately for invalid visibility combinations or hard-filter non-public answers from every machine output path.
Apply the rule consistently to `answers.json`, `llms.txt`, scoped pack outputs, and canonical `/page.md` generation so the same answer cannot leak through a different emitter.
Keep `audit` aligned with the runtime behavior so audit findings and emitted artifacts cannot disagree.
Add regression tests covering `private` and `internal` answers with invalid `ai_visibility` combinations and assert that no machine artifact leaks content.
Acceptance: a non-public answer cannot appear in any machine-facing output unless the product contract explicitly allows it.

tasks.title: Fix mounted base path request routing in the serve gateway
tasks.details: Mounted `base_url` handling in the serve path router is incorrect. It uses prefix string matching and byte slicing that can misroute valid mounted paths and overmatch unrelated prefixes. References: src/cmd/serve.rs:125-143 and 671-674.
steps: Replace raw `starts_with` and substring slicing with path-aware stripping based on the parsed mount path.
Handle root mounts and non-root mounts with the same normalization rules.
Ensure redirect handling, markdown negotiation, and static file lookup all operate on the correctly stripped path.
Add regression tests for mounted base URLs such as `/docs/`, near-prefix collisions such as `/docset/`, and direct `/page.md` fetches under a mount.
Acceptance: `ansorum serve` behaves correctly for mounted sites and does not overmatch sibling prefixes.

tasks.title: Preserve remove events in the file watcher so serve rebuilds on deletions
tasks.details: The serve watcher currently drops file-system remove events because `filter_events` ignores any event whose path no longer exists. That leaves stale pages and assets in memory or on disk until restart. References: src/fs_utils.rs:94-120 and src/cmd/serve.rs:935-969.
steps: Change event filtering so remove events are retained even when the path has already disappeared from disk.
Keep temp-file and ignored-content filtering correct for create/modify/remove cases.
Verify fast rebuild and full recreate flows both handle deletions of markdown files, assets, and directories without leaving stale output behind.
Add focused tests for remove events in `fs_utils` and serve rebuild behavior after deleting content or static files.
Acceptance: deleting a watched file or folder triggers the correct rebuild path and stale output is removed.

tasks.title: Honor delivery configuration for markdown routes and Accept negotiation
tasks.details: The Ansorum delivery config exposes `markdown_routes` and `markdown_negotiation`, but build and serve ignore those switches and always emit `/page.md` plus negotiation behavior. That makes the config misleading and weakens operator control over machine endpoints. References: components/config/src/config/ansorum.rs:339-353, components/site/src/lib.rs:754-790, src/cmd/serve.rs:167-176 and 206-217.
steps: Define the expected contract for `markdown_routes` and `markdown_negotiation` at build time and serve time.
Gate `/page.md` emission on `markdown_routes` and gate `Accept: text/markdown` negotiation on `markdown_negotiation`.
Ensure the config remains internally valid and that docs match the implemented behavior.
Add regression tests for all combinations of the two flags in build outputs and serve responses.
Acceptance: toggling delivery config actually changes emitted artifacts and serve behavior.

tasks.title: Replace serve-thread panics on unexpected I/O with graceful 5xx error handling
tasks.details: Unexpected filesystem errors in the serve path currently panic the request handler instead of returning a server error and logging context. That can crash the serve thread on transient I/O failures. References: src/cmd/serve.rs:505-512.
steps: Replace the panic path in `io_error` with explicit 5xx responses and structured logging.
Preserve `404` and `403` behavior for expected not-found and permission cases.
Make sure the error overlay path still works when a request fails with a server-side I/O error.
Add tests for representative non-NotFound, non-PermissionDenied error kinds if feasible.
Acceptance: unexpected I/O errors no longer panic the server and clients receive a stable error response.

tasks.title: Split answer retrieval aliases from page redirect aliases
tasks.details: The current content model uses one `aliases` field for two incompatible concepts: retrieval/query synonyms and HTML redirect paths. Answer aliases are copied from page aliases, and build renders them as redirect pages. The reference corpus currently turns natural-language retrieval aliases into published redirect URLs. References: components/content/src/front_matter/page.rs:150-170, components/site/src/lib.rs:955-980, test_site_answers/content/refunds.md:1-18.
steps: Design separate fields for answer-query aliases versus URL redirect aliases without losing authored intent.
Update parsing, normalized answer extraction, and runtime emitters so retrieval aliases do not automatically become redirect pages.
Migrate reference fixtures and docs to the new contract.
Add tests covering answer ranking aliases and redirect generation independently.
Acceptance: retrieval synonyms improve answer selection without silently creating URL redirect surfaces.

tasks.title: Replace per-event thread spawning in observability delivery with bounded async dispatch
tasks.details: Observability async mode currently spawns a new OS thread for every emitted event. Under serve traffic, each machine fetch or redirect hit can create another thread if an event hook is configured. References: src/observability.rs:68-102 and src/cmd/serve.rs:175-180.
steps: Design a bounded delivery mechanism such as a background worker, queue, or runtime task model instead of unbounded thread creation.
Preserve sync mode semantics for commands that intentionally block on hook delivery.
Define failure behavior under queue saturation, slow hooks, and process shutdown.
Add tests or targeted verification for event delivery under bursty serve traffic.
Acceptance: async event delivery does not create an unbounded number of OS threads.

tasks.title: Serialize canonical machine markdown front matter safely instead of hand-writing YAML-like text
tasks.details: Canonical `/page.md` output is currently assembled with raw string writes for scalar fields and lists. Values containing YAML-significant characters, multiline text, or other edge cases can corrupt the machine-facing contract. References: components/content/src/page.rs:356-394 and 538-550.
steps: Replace handwritten header generation with a real serializer or an explicitly escaped format contract.
Validate behavior for titles, entities, aliases, and questions containing punctuation, colons, quotes, or newlines.
Keep output deterministic and compatible with the intended machine-markdown contract.
Add regression tests for escaping edge cases in `/page.md` output.
Acceptance: machine markdown remains parseable and stable for authored metadata with special characters.

tasks.title: Implement or remove the dead default_ai_visibility configuration knob
tasks.details: The config surface defines `ansorum.delivery.default_ai_visibility`, but answer parsing still requires explicit `ai_visibility` on answer pages and never applies the configured default. This leaves a dead knob in the product contract. References: components/config/src/config/ansorum.rs:339-364, components/content/src/front_matter/answer.rs:106-119, components/content/src/front_matter/page.rs:150-170.
steps: Decide whether `default_ai_visibility` should apply during answer normalization or be removed from the public config surface.
If retained, apply it consistently only where it does not weaken explicit visibility guarantees.
Update docs and examples so the behavior is unambiguous.
Add regression tests for answer parsing with and without explicit `ai_visibility`.
Acceptance: the configuration is either functional and tested or removed entirely.

tasks.title: Add explicit request timeouts and better failure classification to OpenAI eval calls
tasks.details: The OpenAI eval client currently builds a blocking reqwest client without an explicit timeout. A slow or hung API call can block `ansorum eval` indefinitely. References: src/cmd/eval.rs:352-374.
steps: Add explicit connect and total request timeouts to the eval client.
Classify timeout and transport failures with actionable error messages so operators can distinguish credential, API, and network issues.
Preserve the existing GPT-5.4 validation and response parsing behavior.
Add tests for timeout-related configuration or at least unit coverage for the new error paths.
Acceptance: `ansorum eval --llm` fails predictably under network stalls instead of hanging.

tasks.title: Remove unbounded full-body buffering from serve error overlay injection
tasks.details: The serve error overlay middleware currently reads whole responses into memory with `to_bytes(..., usize::MAX)` before appending the HTML error box. Large pages can cause avoidable memory pressure and latency spikes. References: src/cmd/serve.rs:306-372.
steps: Redesign error overlay injection so it does not require unbounded buffering of entire responses.
If full buffering remains necessary for some cases, add a sane size cap and fail-safe behavior.
Preserve correct handling for normal HTML pages, 404 responses, and WebSocket upgrades.
Add tests or targeted verification for large HTML responses and error overlay behavior.
Acceptance: the error overlay path no longer buffers arbitrarily large bodies without limits.
